pros:
Massive 2.0m² landing zone at only 5kg - exceptional area-to-weight ratio
Packs down to roughly sleeping-bag size, fits inside your main pad or a daypack

Great as a second pad to extend your landing zone without doubling carry weight
cons:
Puncture risk on sharp rock edges or thorny ground - carry the included repair kit
Surface can feel slippery on uneven or dusty terrain - best on flat, even ground
Air compresses all at once rather than progressively - bouncier, less predictable landing feel
No built-in gear storage (no shoe pockets, chalk loops) - carry inside your main pad or a separate bag

Premium price for the build quality
